question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a block tag of '0x0' (hex zero) via the JSON-RPC API, causing findByBlockType() to call recordFileRepository.findByIndex(0) instead of recordFileRepository.findEarliest() due to reference-equality (==) check failing for a non-singleton BlockType instance, resulting in an empty Optional being returned when no record file has index exactly 0, while the true earliest block exists at a different index?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockType with number Long.MAX_VALUE (e.g., '0x7fffffffffffffff') that is not the LATEST singleton, causing findByBlockType() to fall through to recordFileRepository.findByIndex(Long.MAX_VALUE) which always returns empty, silently suppressing the latest block record and causing callers to receive no record file instead of the actual latest?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger incorrect block resolution by passing the string 'safe', 'pending', or 'finalized' as a block tag, causing BlockType.of() to return the LATEST singleton and findByBlockType() to call findLatest(), even though Ethereum semantics for 'safe' and 'finalized' differ from 'latest', resulting in incorrect record file data being returned for historical-state queries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp value that falls in a gap between two consecutive record files (i.e., after consensusEnd of file N but before consensusStart of file N+1), causing findByTimestamp() via the query 'consensusEnd >= ?1' to return file N+1 instead of the correct file, attributing the transaction to the wrong block and exporting incorrect block metadata to mirror node consumers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user pass Long.MAX_VALUE as a timestamp to findByTimestamp(), causing the repository query 'consensusEnd >= Long.MAX_VALUE' to return empty, and callers such as OpcodeServiceImpl to silently fall back to BlockType.LATEST, executing contract debug against the wrong (latest) block state instead of the intended historical state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a null Long timestamp to findByTimestamp(), causing an unhandled NullPointerException when the boxed Long is auto-unboxed to primitive long for the repository call, resulting in a 500 error that leaks stack trace information and disrupts record file resolution for mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp of 0 to findByTimestamp(), causing the query 'consensusEnd >= 0' to return the very first record file regardless of whether any transaction exists at that timestamp, leading to incorrect block attribution in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @CachePut annotation on findByTimestamp() which writes results into the CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX cache keyed by the timestamp parameter (not by the record file's index), causing a subsequent call to findByIndex(T) where T equals a previously used timestamp to return the wrong record file from the poisoned cache entry?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a cache poisoning scenario where findByTimestamp() populates the index cache (CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX) with a record file keyed by a timestamp value that coincidentally matches a valid block index, causing findByBlockType() -> findByIndex(N) to return the wrong cached record file for block N?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reference-equality (==) check for BlockType.EARLIEST by constructing a BlockType with name='earliest' and number=0 through a deserialization path (e.g., JSON @JsonCreator), causing findByBlockType() to skip findEarliest() and call findByIndex(0) instead, returning empty when the genesis block has a non-zero index?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user cause findByBlockType() to return a stale cached record file for BlockType.LATEST because findLatest() is cached with CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_LATEST and the cache TTL has not expired, resulting in mirror node consumers receiving an outdated latest block that omits recently exported transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user cause findByBlockType() to return a stale cached record file for BlockType.EARLIEST because findEarliest() is cached with CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_EARLIEST and never invalidated after the genesis record file is pruned, resulting in mirror node consumers receiving a reference to a deleted record file?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a very large decimal block number (e.g., '9223372036854775807') that parses to Long.MAX_VALUE via BlockType.of(), causing findByBlockType() to call findByIndex(Long.MAX_VALUE) which returns empty, making the mirror node report a missing block for a valid block number query?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a hex block number with leading zeros (e.g., '0x0000000000000001') to BlockType.of(), causing extractNumericBlock() to parse it as index 1 and findByBlockType() to call findByIndex(1), but if the cache already holds a different record file at key 1 from a prior findByTimestamp() call, the wrong record file is returned?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call findByTimestamp() with the same timestamp to exploit the @Cacheable annotation, where the cache stores the first result permanently (unless = '#result == null'), causing mirror node consumers to always receive the same record file even after the underlying data has been corrected or updated?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger incorrect record file resolution by querying with a timestamp equal to the consensusEnd of a record file, where the query 'consensusEnd >= ?1' returns that file, but the actual transaction at that exact timestamp belongs to the next record file due to boundary semantics, causing incorrect block data to be exported?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BlockType.of('') returns LATEST (due to StringUtils.isEmpty check), causing findByBlockType() to call findLatest() when an empty block parameter is supplied, silently resolving to the latest block instead of rejecting the request, potentially causing incorrect historical state queries to execute against the wrong block?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a block number that overflows a long when parsed as hex (e.g., '0xffffffffffffffff'), causing Long.parseLong() to throw an exception that is caught and rethrown as IllegalArgumentException, resulting in a 400/500 error that disrupts record file resolution and causes missing records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user cause findByBlockType() to return an incorrect record file by supplying a block number equal to a timestamp value that was previously cached in the CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX cache by a findByTimestamp() call, exploiting the shared cache namespace between index-based and timestamp-based lookups?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a denial-of-service by flooding findByTimestamp() with unique timestamp values, causing the CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_TIMESTAMP cache to grow unboundedly (if no eviction policy is set), exhausting heap memory and causing mirror node record file exports to fail entirely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a denial-of-service by flooding findByBlockType() with unique large block index values, causing the CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX cache to grow unboundedly, exhausting heap memory and causing all subsequent record file lookups to fail, resulting in missing records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that 'pending' maps to LATEST in BlockType.of(), causing findByBlockType() to return the current latest record file for a 'pending' block query, when Ethereum semantics expect 'pending' to represent the next (not-yet-mined) block, leading to incorrect state being exported to mirror node consumers?
